requirement: Buyer can create an invoice for approved suppliers.
Invoice header must include supplier name, invoice number, invoice date, payment term, currency, control total, delivery number, status, supplier note, attachments, image scan, and chart of accounts.
Invoice can be submitted for approval.
Invoice can be edited in a sequence.
Invoice can be matched with purchase order.
Invoice can be paid.
Invoice can be reported.
Invoice control total can be calculated.
Invoice status can be tracked.
Buyer can create a purchase order for approved suppliers.
Supplier can submit invoices through Coupa Supplier Portal (CSP), SAN/PO Link, AP Mailbox/Invoice Smash (OCR), or Direct Entry.
Supplier can create a PO-backed invoice by flipping a relevant PO into an invoice.
Coupa performs auto-matching of PO, GRN, and Invoice for 3-way matching.

Invoice Line consists of fields such as Line Number, Line Type, Description, Quantity, UOM, Unit Price, Total Price, PO Line, Contract, Supplier Part Number, Commodity, Period, Billing String, and Tax.
WHT Applicable checkbox is defaulted from the supplier profile, and Withholding Taxes are added if applicable on the invoice from the list of WHT configured.
Invoice Fields such as Car Plate ID and Inspection Code are added to capture additional details.
AP Team can create a purchase order-backed invoice directly in Coupa.
Supplier can create a non-PO backed invoice directly in Coupa or via email to AP shared mailbox.
AP Team can review and update invoice details, and request approval from requester and financial manager if required.
AP Team can approve or reject invoices, and dispute invoices if necessary.
Approved invoices are sent to NetSuite for payment processing and payment status is updated in Coupa.
Requester can create a non-PO invoice through invoice forms and submit it in draft status in Coupa.
Accounts Payable can pick the invoice in draft status, review it for completeness, and submit it for approval.
Requester can approve the invoice after reviewing it for legitimacy of spend.
Final AP Clerk can approve the invoice before marking it as ready for payment.
Approved invoice can be sent to NetSuite via integration for payment processing.
NetSuite can process the payment and send the update to Coupa.
Paid invoice status can be reflected in Coupa.
Supplier can flip an invoice to credit note in Coupa.
Supplier can fill in missing details and submit the credit note for review.
AP Team can review credit note details and reject or approve it.
Approved credit note can be sent to NetSuite via integration.
AP Team can void an approved invoice in Coupa.
AP Team can bulk load invoices in Coupa by downloading a template, filling in details, and uploading it.
Buyer can create a standard invoice for approved suppliers.
System checks for compliance fields, duplicate invoice numbers, and invoice date before moving an invoice to Processing status.
System routes non-PO backed invoices to a named Requester user if configured by the supplier.
System routes PO-backed invoices to Pending Action status if a custom submission blocker approval chain is setup.
System moves an invoice to Pending Receipt status if it has progressed through AP Hold check and the applicable match level is 3-way or 3-way-direct and if the invoice is not fully received.
System moves an invoice to On Hold status if it breaches a configured tolerance for its COA or if the supplier records setting Always route invoices from this supplier to approval is enabled.
System moves an invoice to Pending Approval status if a tolerance fails or a custom approval chain is setup to always evaluate the invoice.
System moves an invoice to Approved status if all required approvers have approved the invoice.
System allows invoices in AP Hold, Pending Action, Pending Receipt, On Hold, or Rejected statuses to be disputed and sent to the supplier for corrections.
System allows disputed invoices to be withdrawn from dispute by Accounts Payable or resolved by the supplier.
Invoice can be submitted for approval and will move to Pending Approval status.
Invoice can be approved and will move to Approved status.
Invoice can be rejected and will move to Rejected status.
Invoice can be disputed and will move to Disputed status.
Invoice can be voided by an AP user if it is in Disputed status.
Invoice can be voided by an Admin user if it is in Pending Receipt or Approved status.
Coupa supports matching invoices against purchase orders and receipts.
Coupa requires 3-way matching for all suppliers.
Invoice can have available balances applied as adjustments.
AP user can select and edit multiple invoices in a sequence without exiting the current screen.
AP user can move to the next invoice to edit after saving or submitting an invoice.
Coupa runs invoice tolerances to determine whether invoices can be automatically approved or require further review and approval.
Tolerances for Malaysia will be set at 8% SST variance, while other entities will have tolerances set as per specified rules.
An invoice goes on 'Pending Receipt' or 'On hold' status if it does not satisfy matching or invoice tolerance rules.
Coupa provides two types of invoice tolerance settings: Chart of Accounts-level tolerances and Commodity-level tolerances.
Commodity-level tolerances override Chart of Accounts-level tolerances and apply to all Charts of Accounts.
System blocks non-PO invoice submission without requester details and displays a warning message.
System blocks invoice submission for AP users if invoice tolerance reason code is not added.
System displays a warning message to suppliers for invoices with dates earlier than PO dates.
Coupa provides standard reporting, advanced reporting, and dashboards for users.
Users can export data to Excel, set up custom views, and schedule reports in Coupa.
System compares manually entered invoice totals to calculated totals and displays an error if they differ.
System provides different roles with varying levels of access and functionality.
Configure invoicing tolerance checks with a '0' value tolerance.
Enable group reporting in USD currency.
Enable central receiving role for exception cases.
